title: Difficulty Delivering Shoulders - Shoulder Dystocia
chief_complaint: 35-year-old G2P1 diabetic mother in active labor. Fetal head delivered but shoulders stuck. Turtle sign noted. Baby not delivered after 60 seconds.

physical_exam.delivery: Fetal head delivered, retracted against perineum (turtle sign), shoulders impacted, unable to deliver with gentle downward traction

correct_diagnosis: Shoulder Dystocia (Macrosomic Infant of Diabetic Mother)
key_learning_points: HELPERR mnemonic: Help, Episiotomy, Legs (McRoberts), Pressure (suprapubic), Enter (Rubin/Woods), Remove posterior arm, Roll (Gaskin)
Do NOT: fundal pressure, excessive traction, panic
Neonatal complications: brachial plexus injury, clavicle fracture, HIE
Document time of head delivery and maneuvers

patient_responses.diabetes: I have gestational diabetes on insulin

feedback.correct_message: Excellent! Shoulder dystocia - time is critical.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is shoulder dystocia. Turtle sign is diagnostic.
feedback.attending_note: Shoulder dystocia: Call for help! McRoberts maneuver + suprapubic pressure (first-line, 90% success). Then rotational maneuvers (Rubin II, Woods screw). Gaskin maneuver (all-fours). Document time and maneuvers. Brachial plexus exam of newborn.
